spec_match.rationale: Tries to register `/sw-srcdoc-demo.js` which doesn't exist on the host (static Deno Deploy, no route added). The error path logs `expected — no sw-srcdoc-demo.js in this demo` and then `simulating SW interception behaviour in-page` — so the demo never actually demonstrates fetch interception. The promised behaviour is described, not shown.

interactive.rationale: Real-looking workflow (register, fetch from srcdoc, fetch from src, unregister) drives buttons that update logs and inject srcdoc content. But the SW registration is expected to fail, and the `x-sw-intercepted` header check is fictional — no SW writes that header.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises `In Chrome 135+ the SW intercepts it; pre-135 the fetch bypasses the SW entirely` — but no SW is actually intercepting because no SW is registered. The demo describes the behaviour without exhibiting it.

edge_cases.rationale: Considers no-SW case (the only case that fires), but doesn't demonstrate the actual interception delta — the demo can't because there's no SW file.

references.rationale: Need to verify whether the see-also section includes the chromestatus link (file was only partially read). Add explicitly if missing.

design_system.rationale: Injected srcdoc iframe HTML uses raw hex colours `#059669` (success) and `#e11d48` (error) for inline `style.color`. Per CLAUDE.md rule 5, these should be design-system tokens (`var(--accent-emerald)`, `var(--accent-rose)`). Even in injected iframes, the values authors will copy-paste should match site conventions.

openQuestions.title: Provide a real Service Worker file (or use Blob URL)
openQuestions.detail: The demo registers `/sw-srcdoc-demo.js` which 404s. Either (a) add a server route that serves a real SW for this concept path, (b) use a Blob URL like the srcdoc-sw sibling does, or (c) move this to a manual task and add a real server route per CLAUDE.md guidance for HTTP/header features. Without a real SW, the demo can't actually intercept anything.

openQuestions.title: Wire the x-sw-intercepted readout to something real
openQuestions.detail: The srcdoc fetch checks `r.headers.get('x-sw-intercepted')`. No SW writes that header in this demo. After fixing the SW registration, have the SW respond to fetches with `new Response(body, { headers: { 'x-sw-intercepted': 'true' } })` so the readout is meaningful.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex with design-system variables in injected srcdoc
openQuestions.detail: Inline styles `color: '#059669'` and `color: '#e11d48'` should be `color: var(--accent-emerald)` and `color: var(--accent-rose)`. Note: injected iframes don't share parent stylesheets, so either inject the variable definitions into the iframe head or use computed hex from the design system.

openQuestions.title: Confirm the references section exists
openQuestions.detail: Verify see-also includes chromestatus.com/feature/5128675425779712. If missing, add — required for the cleanup script.

summary: Most ambitious concept of the feature, but the core mechanism (a real SW intercepting srcdoc fetches) doesn't fire — the SW file 404s and the demo falls back to describing the behaviour. Needs a real SW (Blob URL or new server route) to be meaningful.
